user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate the TC387 GTM TOM three-phase inverter PWM to TC4D7 using eGTM ATOM and the IfxEgtm_Pwm API.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 6 complementary, center-aligned PWM outputs for phases U/V/W at 20 kHz with 1.0 µs dead-time, initial duties U=25%, V=50%, W=75%, using eGTM Cluster 1 ATOM1 CH0–CH2, CMU FXCLK_0 for ATOM and CMU CLK0 for DTM, with syncStart and syncUpdate enabled, and a period ISR on CPU0 with priority 20. TOUT pins default to P02.0/7 (U), P02.1/4 (V), P02.2/5 (W) pending final verification.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified high-level IfxEgtm_Pwm driver on TC4D7 to configure eGTM1.ATOM1 channels 0–2 as the base/sync set with DTM-based complementary outputs and 1.0 µs dead-time. Configure center-aligned mode, 20 kHz switching, enable syncStart/syncUpdate, route the proposed TOUT pins via IfxEgtm_PinMap in the driver config, assign CMU clocks (FXCLK_0 for ATOM, CLK0 for DTM) in config, and bind a period ISR (EgtmAtomPeriodIsr) on CPU0 with priority 20 through the driver’s interrupt configuration.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM API supports ATOM with DTM for complementary outputs, center alignment, synchronous start/update, frequency/duty/dead-time updates, and ISR integration. It matches the migration target (TC4xx eGTM) and simplifies channel grouping and synchronization.

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Update the U/V/W duty cycles at runtime with synchronous update enabled while maintaining center-aligned complementary outputs and configured dead-time.

api_changes.reason: TC3xx→TC4xx function name and module migration
TC3xx→TC4xx function name and module migration
ISR name transform for eGTM/ATOM period interrupt on CPU0
Watchdog module migration; exact API differs by template
Safety/system watchdog naming change on TC4xx

migration_notes.context_preserved: The migration keeps the exact PWM behavior: eGTM1.ATOM1 CH0–CH2, center-aligned complementary pairs with 1.0 µs hardware DTM dead-time, 20 kHz switching, initial U/V/W duties 25/50/75%, synchronous start and update, and a period ISR on CPU0 at priority 20. The unified IfxEgtm_Pwm driver configures DTM, clocks (FXCLK_0 for ATOM, CLK0 for DTM), pin routing via IfxEgtm_PinMap within the config, and ISR binding without exposing low-level setup calls, matching the original intent on the new TC4D7.

PWM.rationale: Driver locked by refiner node (user explicit or engine-scored)
